properties.remark: *** 2 INJ *** AN EF1 TORNADO TRACKED FOR 12.8 MILES THROUGH REPUBLIC COUNTY. THE FIRST INSTANCE OF DAMAGE WAS 2 MILES NORTH NORTHWEST OF BELLEVILLE AT APPROXIMATELY 650 PM. IT TRACKED TO THE EAST NORTHEAST THROUGH THE TOWN OF MUNDEN BEFORE ENDING 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF NARKA KANSAS AT 712 PM. THE PEAK WIND WAS ESTIMATED AT 110 MPH.

MHK AIRPORT TOWER AND AIRLINE PILOT REPORT THAT A WEAK FUNNEL CONTACTED THE GROUND FOR LESS THAN 2 MILES. THERE WAS NO VISIBLE DEBRIS OR DAMAGE AND IT OCCURRED OVER OPEN COUNTRY. THIS TORNADO CAME FROM A CONVECTIVE SHOWER WITH NO EVIDENCE OF LIGHTNING.
